LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AFTER ARGUMENTS, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS MODIFIED: DEFENDANT PATRICK STRATEMAN'S MOTION TO DISMISS OR TO STAY ACTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. MOVING DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO SHOW THAT NEW ZEALAND IS A SUITABLE FORUM. IT HAS NOT BEEN SHOWN THAT DEFENDANTS INTERSANGO LTD, DONALD NORMAN, OR AMIR TAAKI ARE SUBJECT TO NEW ZEALAND JURISDICTION. ADDITIONALLY, DEFENDANT HAS PRESENTED NO EVIDENCE AS TO THE APPLICABLE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS IN NEW ZEALAND AND WHETHER PLAINTIFFS' CLAIMS ARE TIME BARRED IN THAT FORUM. EVEN IF A SUITABLE FORUM DID EXIST, THE PRIVATE AND PUBLIC INTEREST FACTORS DO NOT WARRANT GRANTING THE MOTION. AS TO THE PRIVATE FACTORS: PLAINTIFFS DID NOT AGREE TO ADJUDICATE ALL DISPUTES IN NEW ZEALAND. THE LANGUAGE OF THE TERMS AND CONDITIONS ALLEGEDLY ON THE WEBSITE, EVEN IF IT IS ENFORCEABLE, STATES ONLY THAT "YOU [MEANING A CUSTOMER] AGREE TO SUBMIT TO THE PERSONAL AND EXCLUSIVE JURISDICTION OF THE COURT LOCATED WITHIN NEW ZEALAND TO SETTLE ANY DISPUTE," NOT THAT CUSTOMERS COULD NOT FILE ACTIONS AGAINST BITCOINICA OUTSIDE NEW ZEALAND. NONE OF THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS RESIDE IN NEW ZEALAND. THERE HAS BEEN NO SHOWING THAT ACCESS TO EVIDENCE REQUIRES MOVING THE CASE TO NEW ZEALAND. DEFENDANT STRATEMAN APPARENTLY ACCESSED RELEVANT DOCUMENTS REGARDING CLAIMS WHILE IN CALIFORNIA. (SEE POWELL DECL. IN OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO QUASH AND SKYP CONVERSATION..) CALIFORNIA HAS A STRONG INTEREST IN ASSURING A FORUM FOR CALIFORNIA RESIDENTS. TWO OF THE FOUR PLAINTIFFS RESIDE IN CALIFORNIA. THE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE BASED UPON CONTRACT, COMMON COUNT, AND TORT LIABILITY. MOVING DEFENDANTS' ARGUMENT THAT THE CALIFORNIA COURTS ARE OVERBURDENED IS NOT, UNDER ALL OF THE CIRCUMSTANCES, A BASIS TO MOVE THIS CASE FILED BY CALIFORNIA RESIDENTS TO NEW ZEALAND. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PATRICK STRATEMAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 15 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION (NEGLIGENCE) ON THE GROUND OF UNCERTAINTY. CCP 430.10(F). IT IS NOT CERTAIN WHETHER PLAINTIFFS ARE ALLEGING A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE OR FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY, AND THE NATURE OF THE ALLEGED WRONGFUL ACT BY DEFENDANT STRATEMAN UNDER CIVIL CODE 2343 OR OTHERWISE. THE DEMURRER TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONVERSION IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFFS HAVE SUFFICIENTLY PLED THE ELEMENTS OF CONVERSION, INCLUDING THE DOLLAR AMOUNT OF THEIR CLAIM. (COMPLAINT 16). THE ANALOGY TO A CREDITOR-DEBTOR RELATIONSHIP IS DISTINGUISHABLE. (CF. METROPOLITAN LIFE INS. CO. V. SAN FRANCISCO BANK (1943), 58 CAL. APP. 2D 528.) THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT BITCOINICA IS A CURRENCY EXCHANGE. COMPLAINT 10-14. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-10-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ELLEN P. LIU, ESQ., 415.392.1960, FOR DEFENDANT PATRICK STRATEMAN; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT PATRICK STRATEMAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with 15 days leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action (negligence) on the ground of uncertainty. CCP 430.10(f). It is not certain whether Plaintiffs are alleging a cause of action for negligence or for breach of fiduciary duty, and the nature of the alleged wrongful act by Defendant Strateman under Civil Code 2343 or otherwise. The demurrer to the fifth cause of action for conversion is overruled. Plaintiffs have sufficiently pled the elements of conversion, including the dollar amount of their claim. (Complaint 16). The analogy to a creditor-debtor relationship is distinguishable. (Cf. Metropolitan Life Ins. Co. v. San Francisco Bank (1943), 58 Cal. App. 2d 528.) The complaint alleges that Bitcoinica is a currency exchange. Complaint 10-14. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-06-2013 Continued To Apr-10-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT PATRICK STRATEMAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is continued on the court's own motion to April 10, 2013 to give Mr. Strateman the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Mr. Strateman must deliver courtesy copies of its moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than March 6, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be overruled. The court has courtesy copies of the opposition and reply papers. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Process For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Jan-25-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Gilda R. Turitz, Esq. of Sideman & Bancroft LLP, Tel. 415.686.0580, for defendants and moving party;
Henry Y. Chiu, Esq. of Moss Tucker Chiu Hebesha & Ward, Tel. 559.472.9922 for plaintiff and opposing party by court call.

Judge Miller disclosed that she knows Miss Turitz and had attended functions with her and that this should not affect her ability to be fair and impartial in this case. No objections were raised.

RULING: The court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. SPECIALLY APPEARING DEFENDANT INTERSANGO LTD.'S Motion To Quash Service Of Process For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction is Granted. Plaintiff failed to produce facts showing Defendant Intersango, Ltd has any California contacts relevant to this case. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dimsiss Or To Stay Action; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Jan-25-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Gilda R. Turitz, Esq. of Sideman & Bancroft LLP, Tel. 415.686.0580, for defendants and moving party;
Henry Y. Chiu, Esq. of Moss Tucker Chiu Hebesha & Ward, Tel. 559.472.9922 for plaintiff and opposing party by court call.

Judge Miller disclosed that she knows Miss Turitz and had attended functions with her and that this should not affect her ability to be fair and impartial in this case. No objections were raised.

RULING: A R G U E D. After arguments, the court adopted its tentative ruling as modified: DEFENDANT PATRICK STRATEMAN'S Motion To Dismiss Or To Stay Action is denied without prejudice. Moving Defendant haS failed to show that New Zealand is a suitable forum. It has not been shown that Defendants Intersango Ltd, Donald Norman, or Amir Taaki are subject to New Zealand jurisdiction. Additionally, Defendant has presented no evidence as to the applicable statute of limitations in New Zealand and whether Plaintiffs' claims are time barred in that forum. Even if a suitable forum did exist, the private and public interest factors do not warrant granting the motion. As to the private factors: Plaintiffs did not agree to adjudicate all disputes in New Zealand. The language of the Terms and Conditions allegedly on the website, even if it is enforceable, states only that "you [meaning a customer] agree to submit to the personal and exclusive jurisdiction of the court located within New Zealand to settle any dispute," not that customers could not file actions against Bitcoinica outside New Zealand. None of the individual defendants reside in New Zealand. There has been no showing that access to evidence requires moving the case to New Zealand. Defendant Strateman apparently accessed relevant documents regarding claims while in California. (See Powell Decl. in opposition to motion to quash and Skyp conversation..) California has a strong interest in assuring a forum for California residents. Two of the four plaintiffs reside in California. The causes of action are based upon contract, common count, and tort liability. Moving Defendants' argument that the California courts are overburdened is not, under all of the circumstances, a basis to move this case filed by California residents to New Zealand. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS INTERSANGO LTD. and PATRICK STRATEMAN'S Motion To Quash Service Of Process For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction is continued to January 4, 2013, to give opposing party an opportunity to provide courtesy copies of the opposing papers as required by Local Rule 2.6B. The opposing party shall provide the opposition papers with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date by December 18, 2012. The Court has courtesy copies of the moving papers and reply. Judge: Marla Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
